expectations.text: Output contains a structured audit report table with columns including Issue, Severity, and Fix
expectations.evidence: The audit-report.md contains a 20-row markdown table with columns: #, Issue, Severity, Location, Fix. All three required columns (Issue, Severity, Fix) are present with substantive content in every row.

expectations.text: Report evaluates all 5 audit dimensions: dependencies, modernity, performance, conflict, consistency
expectations.evidence: The 'Dimension Analysis' section (lines 38-88) contains all five subsections: DEPENDENCIES (verdict: FAILED), MODERNITY (verdict: PRE-2020 LEVEL), PERFORMANCE (verdict: N/A), CONFLICT (verdict: NO CONFLICTS), CONSISTENCY (verdict: N/A). Each has a multi-paragraph analysis.

expectations.text: CRITICAL issues are identified (no GSAP loaded, no scroll animations, no reduced-motion guard)
expectations.evidence: Issue 1: 'No GSAP library loaded' (CRITICAL). Issue 2: 'No ScrollTrigger plugin loaded — scroll-driven animations impossible' (CRITICAL). Issue 4: 'No prefers-reduced-motion guard — accessibility requirement is unmet' (CRITICAL). All three specific issues named in the expectation are present and marked CRITICAL.

expectations.text: Summary line states count of CRITICAL, WARNING, and INFO items
expectations.evidence: Line 34 of audit-report.md: '**Summary:** 7 critical, 9 warnings, 4 info'. All three severity levels are counted. The counts match the actual table rows (7 CRITICAL rows #1-7, 9 WARNING rows #8-16, 4 INFO rows #17-20).

expectations.text: Concrete Before/After fix patches are provided for CRITICAL and WARNING items
expectations.evidence: Seven Before/After code patches are provided in the 'Proposed Changes' section (Fixes #1-#7). All 7 CRITICAL issues are covered by Fixes #1-#5 (some grouped). Two WARNING items are covered by Fixes #6 (hero entrance, issue 8) and #7 (card hover/reveal, issue 9). The remaining 7 WARNING items have inline fix descriptions in the table's Fix column but lack full Before/After patches. The expectation is satisfied for the CRITICAL category and partially for WARNING, with patches present for both severity levels.

expectations.text: Each fix has a Why explanation sentence
expectations.evidence: All 7 Proposed Changes include a '**Why:**' paragraph: Fix #1 ('Without GSAP loaded, zero animations can execute'), Fix #2 ('All animations must be wrapped in a matchMedia guard'), Fix #3 ('ScrollTrigger calculates trigger positions based on element positions'), Fix #4 ('The cursor and ring elements are in the DOM positioned at (0,0)'), Fix #5 ('The marquee has two duplicate content spans'), Fix #6 ('The hero section is the first thing visitors see'), Fix #7 ('The 8 bento grid cards are the primary content').

expectations.text: Report identifies that the page has NO animation JavaScript code despite having animation-related CSS classes
expectations.evidence: Issue 3: 'No animation JavaScript at all — page is 100% static; zero scroll reveals, zero micro-interactions, zero entrance animations' (CRITICAL). MODERNITY section: 'This page has zero animations of any kind'. Overall Assessment: 'well-structured, animation-ready HTML template that currently has zero animation implementation. The HTML uses semantic class names (.hero-badge, .magnetic-btn, .section-label, .marquee-track, .card) that map directly to GSAP animation patterns... However, the JavaScript layer is entirely absent.' DEPENDENCIES: 'The HTML structure references animation concepts extensively (magnetic buttons, custom cursor, marquee, scroll-driven storytelling), but none of the required JavaScript exists.'

claims.evidence: Consistently reported as 980 lines in transcript, audit-report.md header, and metrics.json file_stats.input_lines

claims.claim: The page has zero animation JavaScript code
claims.evidence: metrics.json confirms animations_found: 0, gsap_scripts_found: 0, css_transitions_found: 0, css_keyframes_found: 0. Multiple report sections corroborate this finding.

claims.claim: The severity table has 20 issues (7 critical, 9 warning, 4 info)
claims.evidence: Counted 20 numbered rows in the audit table. Rows 1-7 are CRITICAL, 8-16 are WARNING, 17-20 are INFO. Summary line matches: '7 critical, 9 warnings, 4 info'.

claims.claim: Every After code patch is copy-paste-ready with no placeholder text
claims.evidence: Reviewed all 7 Before/After patches. Each contains concrete GSAP code with specific selectors, durations, easing values, and CDN URLs. No '[placeholder]' or 'TODO' text found. Fix #1 has a comment '// Animation code goes here' which is a structural comment, not a placeholder.

claims.claim: The self-clarity check verified CRITICAL count matches actual CRITICAL rows
claims.evidence: Summary says 7 critical. Table rows #1-7 are all marked CRITICAL. The count is accurate.
Transcript lists 3 files read: SKILL.md, index.html, audit-rules.md. metrics.json confirms files_read: 3.

suggestions.assertion: Concrete Before/After fix patches are provided for CRITICAL and WARNING items
suggestions.reason: This assertion passes even though only 2 of 9 WARNING items received full Before/After patches (the other 7 WARNINGs have only inline fix descriptions in the table). Consider tightening to 'Before/After patches are provided for ALL CRITICAL and WARNING items' or specifying a minimum coverage threshold.

No assertion checks whether the fix patches are syntactically valid JavaScript/HTML. A patch with incorrect GSAP API usage (wrong method names, missing required parameters) would still pass all current assertions. Consider adding a programmatic syntax check or at least an assertion that patches reference correct GSAP methods.

No assertion checks that the dimension analysis verdicts are reasonable given the page state. The report correctly identifies N/A for Performance and Consistency (since no animations exist), but an incorrect verdict like 'PASSED' for Dependencies would go undetected by the current assertion set.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions provide good coverage of report structure and key content. The main gap is depth-checking: assertions verify presence of elements (table, dimensions, patches, Why sentences) but not correctness of their content. The WARNING patch coverage assertion could be more precise.
